data.title: Microservices Infrastructure
data.description: Spotify's architecture is built on microservices, where each service is responsible for specific functionality, allowing for greater scalability and agility.
data.technical_spec: Service-oriented architecture using RESTful APIs and gRPC for communication.

data.title: Data Infrastructure
data.description: Utilizes a robust data infrastructure to process and analyze real-time data, supporting features like Discover Weekly and Wrapped.
data.technical_spec: Uses Hadoop, Cassandra, and Google Cloud Platform for data storage and processing.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Employs CDNs to distribute content efficiently across global data centers, ensuring low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Uses providers like Fastly or Akamai to cache content closer to users.

data.title: Cloud-native Deployment
data.description: Leverages containerization and orchestration tools for efficient deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Kubernetes and Docker are essential components for deployment automation.

data.title: APIs for Integration
data.description: Provides a set of comprehensive APIs for developers to integrate Spotify's music streaming capabilities into third-party applications.
data.technical_spec: Publicly available RESTful APIs with robust documentation.
